profile.description: Short baseline scan for the supplied targets.

findings.title: Website Does Not Use HTTPS
findings.description: The target website is using HTTP instead of HTTPS.
findings.recommendation: Use HTTPS with a valid TLS/SSL certificate.
findings.evidence: The target website is using HTTP instead of HTTPS.

findings.title: Missing Security Header: Strict-Transport-Security
findings.description: HSTS header is missing. This helps enforce HTTPS connections.
findings.evidence: HSTS header is missing. This helps enforce HTTPS connections.

findings.title: Missing Security Header: Content-Security-Policy
findings.description: Content Security Policy is missing. This may increase XSS risk.
findings.evidence: Content Security Policy is missing. This may increase XSS risk.

findings.title: Missing Security Header: X-Frame-Options
findings.description: X-Frame-Options header is missing. The site may be vulnerable to clickjacking.
findings.evidence: X-Frame-Options header is missing. The site may be vulnerable to clickjacking.

findings.title: Missing Security Header: Permissions-Policy
findings.recommendation: Add Permissions-Policy to limit browser feature access.

findings.title: Information Disclosure in Header: Server
findings.description: The response exposes server technology information: Server: CyberScanWeb/1.0 Python/3.13.2
findings.recommendation: Remove or minimize technology/version information from HTTP response headers.

findings.title: Form Uses GET Method: Form #1
findings.description: A form uses the GET method. Sensitive data may appear in URLs.
findings.recommendation: Use POST for forms that send sensitive information.

findings.title: Password Form Without HTTPS: Form #1
findings.description: A password field was found on a non-HTTPS page.
findings.recommendation: Always use HTTPS for login and password forms.

findings.title: Sensitive Input Allows Browser Autocomplete: Form #1
findings.description: The input 'password' may allow browser autocomplete for sensitive data.
findings.recommendation: Disable autocomplete for sensitive inputs or use context-appropriate autocomplete values.

findings.title: Possible Error Message Disclosure
findings.description: The page contains possible technical error text: stack trace
findings.recommendation: Disable detailed error messages in production.

findings.title: SQL Injection Review Candidate: Form #1
findings.description: The form contains fields commonly connected to database lookup or authentication logic.
findings.recommendation: Validate input server-side and use parameterized queries for all database access.

findings.title: Possible DOM XSS Source-to-Sink Pattern
findings.description: Inline JavaScript appears to read browser-controlled input and write to risky HTML/script sinks.
findings.recommendation: Use safe DOM APIs such as textContent, sanitize HTML with a proven sanitizer, and avoid eval-like execution.
findings.evidence: Sources: location.hash; sinks: innerHTML, insertAdjacentHTML, setTimeout(, setInterval(

findings.title: Stored XSS Review Candidate
findings.description: The page contains user-content style inputs that may later be rendered back to other users.
findings.recommendation: HTML-encode stored user content by output context, sanitize rich text, and add regression tests for stored XSS.

findings.title: Password Field Missing Explicit Autocomplete Context
findings.description: A password input does not declare current-password or new-password autocomplete context.
findings.recommendation: Use autocomplete=current-password for login forms and autocomplete=new-password for registration or reset forms.

findings.title: Client-Side Authentication Surface Detected
findings.description: The page or bundled script references authentication tokens, login, registration, or password flows.
findings.recommendation: Review token storage, password reset protections, lockout/rate limiting, and session expiration server-side.
